name: Lagoa da Lucenza Route
name_original: Alto de Couto – circular desde A Seara por la cascada do Fondo da Petada y el Pico Formigueiros
description: Hike the difficult 10.7-mile Lagoa da Lucenza Route in Serra do Courel, featuring a glacial lake, Pico Formigueiros, and waterfalls.
region_intro: Hiking around Quiroga offers diverse trails within the Montañas do Courel Geopark and the wider Ribeira Sacra area of Galicia, Spain. The region features a landscape characterized by deep valleys, rivers, and significant geological formations. Hikers can explore tranquil forests, glacial lakes, and numerous waterfalls, with elevations ranging from gentle paths to challenging ascents. This varied terrain provides opportunities for different experience levels, showcasing the area's rich natural beauty. Best hiking trails around Quiroga The most popular hiking route is Lagoa da Lucenza Route, a difficult 10.7 miles (17.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route features a glacial lake and the notable Pico Formigueiros. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of Gold and Iron, a difficult 10.3 miles (16.5 km) path. This trail allows exploration of the area's mining history and cultural landmarks. Local hikers also love the Devesa da Rogueira – Deer Spring Waterfall loop from Alto do Couto, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through tranquil forests and past a waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Quiroga is defined by deep valleys, rivers, and diverse geological formations within the Montañas do Courel Geopark. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest walks to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Quiroga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 260 hikers have used komoot to explore Quiroga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Quiroga?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails in Quiroga, offering diverse options for all experience levels. The region features 19 easy routes, 12 moderate, and 7 difficult trails, ensuring a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Quiroga?
A: Yes, Quiroga offers several family-friendly options. Many of the 19 easy routes are suitable for families, providing gentle paths through tranquil forests and scenic areas within the Montañas do Courel Geopark.

Q: Can I find hikes that feature waterfalls in Quiroga?
A: Yes, Quiroga is known for its beautiful waterfalls. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31696248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador das Gurbias – Vieiros Waterfall loop from A Seara&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devesa da Rogueira – Deer Spring Waterfall loop from Alto do Couto&lt;/a&gt;, which leads past the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Spring Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Quiroga?
A: Yes, many trails in Quiroga are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devesa da Rogueira – Deer Spring Waterfall loop from Alto do Couto&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31696248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador das Gurbias – Vieiros Waterfall loop from A Seara&lt;/a&gt; offers a more challenging circular experience.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails in Quiroga?
A: Quiroga's trails are rich in natural beauty. You can discover features like the glacial lake on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979709118/lagoa-da-lucenza-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoa da Lucenza Route&lt;/a&gt;, deep valleys, and the diverse geological formations of the Montañas do Courel Geopark. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Spring Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pena de Matacás viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I explore the region's mining history through hiking?
A: Yes, Quiroga offers a unique opportunity to explore its mining heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983304461/route-of-gold-and-iron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of Gold and Iron&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 10.3-mile hike within the Montañas do Courel Geopark that allows you to discover the area's historical mining sites and cultural landmarks.

Q: What is the Montañas do Courel Geopark?
A: The Montañas do Courel Geopark is a significant natural area in Quiroga, renowned for its rich geological formations, diverse flora and fauna, and unique ecosystems. Many of Quiroga's hiking trails traverse this geopark, offering stunning landscapes and insights into the region's natural history.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Quiroga?
A: The trails in Quiroga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from tranquil forests to challenging mountain ascents, and the stunning natural beauty of the Montañas do Courel Geopark.

Q: Are there any specific peaks or viewpoints to aim for on a hike?
A: For those seeking panoramic views, peaks like Cerro Negro offer rewarding vistas of the surrounding countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979709118/lagoa-da-lucenza-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoa da Lucenza Route&lt;/a&gt; also features the notable Pico Formigueiros, providing challenging climbs and scenic rewards.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to see near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the trails, you can visit landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2808011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fox Cave&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2969970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5014945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Caldelas Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which are all within the wider Quiroga area.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or through deep valleys?
A: Yes, the region of Quiroga is characterized by its rivers and deep valleys. Many trails, such as those within the Montañas do Courel Geopark, offer scenic walks alongside waterways like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6560211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lor River&lt;/a&gt;, providing picturesque views and a refreshing hiking experience.
tips: Beautiful and spectacular stream of water that falls into the Selmo river, in Vieiros, right in the heart of Serra do Courel.
Viewpoint located on the right bank of the river, in the center of A Seara, with views of the small waterfalls that form in the Selmo river as it passes through this small village.

Waterfalls located at an altitude of 1264 meters in Folgoso do Courel that fall into Rego da Rogueira. The waterfalls present a curious bicolor appearance due to the wear of the wall from which they descend in parallel with different types of water: crystalline waters and ferruginous waters.

At the highest point of the Courel mountain range is the Formigueiros peak, at an altitude of 1,639 meters. The ascent through low difficulty trails leads to the top where you can see a beautiful panoramic view of Teso das Papoulas, Pico Polín and Devesa da Rogueira.

faqs.question: Is the Lagoa da Lucenza Route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered to be of medium difficulty. It involves a continuous ascent with some strenuous sections and a significant elevation gain. While it's a rewarding experience, it's not ideal for beginners or very young children due to its challenging nature and mountain terrain. Hikers should be in good physical condition.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Lagoa da Lucenza Route?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts from the picturesque village of A Seara. You can typically find parking available within or near the village, which serves as the main access point for this route.

faqs.question: Is the Lagoa da Lucenza Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail. However, as it's a mountain route, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to ensure they are capable of handling the strenuous terrain and distance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are often recommended for hiking the Lagoa da Lucenza Route due to more benign weather and vibrant scenery. While beautiful, winter hiking requires extra preparation due to potential snow, ice, and extreme weather conditions. Summer can be pleasant but may involve warmer temperatures.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in the Serra do Courel UNESCO Global Geopark?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Lagoa da Lucenza Route or to access the Serra do Courel UNESCO Global Geopark. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice leave no trace principles to preserve this natural heritage.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and medium difficulty, we strongly recommend bringing appropriate gear. This includes sturdy mountain boots, sufficient water, provisions, and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also advisable, even though the trail is generally well-marked.

faqs.question: What natural highlights will I see along the Lagoa da Lucenza Route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning natural beauty. Key highlights include the unique glacial Pico Formigueiros, the highest peak in the O Courel area, and several waterfalls such as the Vieiros Waterfall and Deer Spring Waterfall. You'll also enjoy panoramic views from spots like Mirador das Gurbias.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Lagoa da Lucenza Route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, transitioning from the traditional village surroundings of A Seara to forested valleys and high-altitude heathlands. You'll encounter continuous ascents, rocky paths, and potentially muddy sections, especially after rain. The path is a typical mountain trail, requiring good footing.

faqs.question: Is the Lagoa da Lucenza Route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Lagoa da Lucenza Route (PR-G 184) is a circular hiking trail. It starts and ends in the village of A Seara, allowing you to experience the full diversity of the landscape without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for hiking the Lagoa da Lucenza Route?
faqs.answer: Starting early in the day is advisable to allow ample time, especially given the 4 to 4.5-hour estimated duration. Be prepared for significant elevation changes and ensure you have enough water, as natural sources might not always be reliable or accessible. The landscape changes dramatically with seasons, so check weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Does the Lagoa da Lucenza Route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily following the Ruta da Lagoa da Lucenza, the route also passes through sections of the Ruta da Devesa da Rogueira and the Camiño do Teso, offering glimpses into the wider network of trails in the Serra do Courel.
